mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in >65% of periods when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that immediately trigger risk-off positioning, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >70% of triggered instances, or the signal fails to separate bull/bear outcomes with p < 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, which drives corporate revenue growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields negative 60-day returns in >60% of occurrences, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample regression.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production indicates strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which translates into higher corporate profit margins and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration coincides with negative 20-day SPX returns in >55% of cases, or the predictive alpha decays to zero within 10 trading days.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index growth surpassing 3.0 percent annually forces equity markets to price in imminent monetary tightening, causing immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the following 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 5-day returns in >65% of high-inflation trigger months, or the signal shows no predictive power after controlling for VIX levels.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market expansion, which supports consumer spending and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal produces negative 20-day returns in >50% of instances, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product growth reflects expanding economic activity and corporate earnings potential, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day investment horizon.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth fails to predict 60-day SPX direction with a Rank IC above 0.04, or positive GDP growth coincides with negative equity returns in >60% of quarters.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production exceeding 0.5 percent signals a sudden contraction in manufacturing activity, triggering immediate risk aversion and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 5-day returns in >70% of triggered periods, or the threshold signal loses predictive accuracy when VIX exceeds 25.
